footer.description: Crafting authentic Japanese ramen with passion since 2015. Every bowl tells a story of tradition and dedication.

signature.title: Signature Bowls
signature.subtitle: Our most beloved creations, perfected over the years

items.name: Tonkotsu Classic
items.description: Rich, creamy pork bone broth simmered for 18 hours with thin noodles, chashu pork belly, soft-boiled egg, and scallions.

items.name: Spicy Miso Ramen
items.description: Bold miso broth with chili oil, ground pork, corn, bean sprouts, butter, and wavy noodles.

items.name: Shoyu Ramen
items.description: Clear soy sauce-based broth with chicken, bamboo shoots, nori, naruto fish cake, and straight noodles.

items.name: Black Garlic Tonkotsu
items.description: Our signature tonkotsu elevated with roasted black garlic oil, wood ear mushrooms, and extra chashu.

seasonal.title: Seasonal Specials
seasonal.subtitle: Limited-time offerings inspired by the season

items.name: Yuzu Shio Ramen
items.description: Light salt-based broth brightened with fresh yuzu citrus, topped with seared chicken and spring vegetables.

items.name: Tantanmen
items.description: Creamy sesame broth with Sichuan peppercorn, seasoned minced pork, bok choy, and chili threads.

sides.title: Sides & Small Plates
sides.subtitle: The perfect companions to your ramen

items.name: Gyoza (6 pcs)
items.description: Pan-fried pork and cabbage dumplings served with ponzu dipping sauce.

items.name: Karaage Chicken
items.description: Crispy Japanese fried chicken marinated in ginger and soy, served with kewpie mayo.

items.name: Edamame
items.description: Steamed young soybeans tossed with sea salt and a hint of chili flakes.

items.name: Takoyaki (6 pcs)
items.description: Golden octopus balls drizzled with takoyaki sauce, kewpie mayo, and bonito flakes.

items.name: Japanese Draft Beer
items.description: Crisp Asahi or Sapporo on tap, the perfect ramen companion.

items.name: Sake Flight
items.description: Three curated sakes: junmai, ginjo, and daiginjo from renowned breweries.

items.name: Yuzu Lemonade
items.description: House-made sparkling lemonade with fresh yuzu citrus and a hint of honey.

items.name: Matcha Latte
items.description: Ceremonial-grade matcha whisked with steamed oat milk, served hot or iced.

ourStory.title: Our Story
ourStory.subtitle: A passion for perfect ramen
ourStory.originP1: Ichiban Ramen was born from a simple dream: to bring the authentic flavors of Japan’s finest ramen shops to New York City. Our founder, Chef Takeshi Yamamoto, spent two years apprenticing under legendary ramen masters in Tokyo and Fukuoka, learning the art and science of crafting the perfect bowl.
ourStory.originP2: What started as a weekend pop-up at Brooklyn food markets quickly became the talk of the city. Lines stretched around the block as word spread about our impossibly rich tonkotsu broth and handmade noodles. In 2015, we opened our doors on Noodle Street, and we haven’t looked back since.
ourStory.originP3: Today, Ichiban Ramen is more than a restaurant. It’s a gathering place where families, friends, and ramen lovers come together over steaming bowls of comfort, united by the belief that great food brings people together.
ourStory.chefAlt: Chef Takeshi Yamamoto preparing ramen in the kitchen
ourStory.brothText: Our tonkotsu broth is the soul of every bowl. We simmer premium pork bones for a minimum of 18 hours at a rolling boil, extracting every ounce of collagen and flavor. The result is a broth so rich and creamy it coats the noodles like silk. We never take shortcuts, never use concentrates, and never compromise. Each batch is tasted and approved by Chef Yamamoto himself before a single bowl is served.
ourStory.noodlesText: We make our noodles fresh every morning using a custom blend of premium Japanese flour, kansui mineral water, and a technique passed down through generations. Each style of ramen gets its own noodle: thin and straight for tonkotsu, wavy and chewy for miso, and medium-width for shoyu. The right noodle transforms a good bowl into an unforgettable one, and we obsess over getting it perfect every single day.
ourStory.milestonesSubtitle: Key milestones in the Ichiban story

testimonials.quote: The best ramen I've had outside of Japan. The tonkotsu broth is absolutely transcendent.
testimonials.author: Sarah M.

testimonials.quote: Every bowl feels like a warm hug. The Black Garlic Tonkotsu is life-changing.
testimonials.author: James K.

testimonials.quote: Incredible attention to detail. You can taste the 18 hours in every spoonful of broth.
testimonials.author: Emily R.

milestones.title: The Journey Begins
milestones.description: Chef Takeshi Yamamoto travels to Japan to apprentice under ramen masters in Tokyo and Fukuoka.

milestones.title: Brooklyn Pop-Up
milestones.description: First Ichiban Ramen pop-up at Brooklyn food markets draws lines around the block.

milestones.title: Grand Opening
milestones.description: Ichiban Ramen opens its permanent home at 742 Noodle Street, Brooklyn.

milestones.title: Critical Acclaim
milestones.description: Named "Best Ramen in NYC" by New York Magazine and featured on the Cooking Channel.

milestones.title: Community Spirit
milestones.description: Launched a free ramen program, serving 10,000+ bowls to frontline workers during the pandemic.

milestones.title: A Decade of Ramen
milestones.description: Celebrating 10 years of serving Brooklyn with plans for a second location in Manhattan.
